dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The metallic tang of blood filled my mouth, the screech of tires still echoing in my ears. Then, nothing. Just the familiar, suffocating darkness.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: GASP!

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: My eyes snapped open. The fluorescent lights of the classroom hummed above me. The scent of dust and old textbooks filled my nostrils. This again.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat against the silence of the early morning classroom. The clock on the wall read 7:58 AM. Two minutes before the bell.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: Another rewind. Another failure. This time, it was the crosswalk. Minjun, distracted, stepping out right as the delivery truck rounded the corner. A blur of red, then…

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: I clenched my fists, nails digging into my palms. How many times? How many deaths would it take before I could save him?

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: I pushed myself up from my desk, my movements stiff. My gaze immediately swept across the room, searching. He wasn't here yet.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: FOOTSTEPS (distant)

dialogue.speaker: Kang Minjun
dialogue.line: Hey, Jinwoo! You're early today.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: Minjun. His smile, bright and carefree, was a dagger to my chest. He had no idea. No idea of the loops, the deaths, the burden.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: Just… trying to get a head start on homework.

dialogue.speaker: Kang Minjun
dialogue.line: Seriously? You're such a goodie-two-shoes. Come on, let's grab some coffee from the vending machine before class starts. My treat.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: No! Not the vending machine. That’s where… no, that was a different loop. But still, the hallway is always a risk.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: Uh, actually, I forgot my wallet. I'll just… stay here.

dialogue.speaker: Kang Minjun
dialogue.line: Don't be silly, I said my treat! Come on, it'll only take a minute. I saw some new flavors.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: Minjun grabbed my arm, pulling me towards the door. My heart pounded. I had to keep him safe, but how? Every time I tried to change something, another variable emerged.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: We walked down the bustling hallway. Students laughed, chatted, oblivious. I scanned every face, every corner, every loose tile. The crosswalk, the rooftop, the chemistry lab… all places he'd died before.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: Minjun was already at the vending machine, excitedly pointing at a new drink. I stood a few feet back, my eyes darting around.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: CREAK (from above)

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: My head snapped up. A maintenance panel in the ceiling directly above Minjun was loose. It swayed precariously.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: Minjun! Get back!

dialogue.speaker: Kang Minjun
dialogue.line: Huh? What are you—

dialogue.speaker: THUD! CRASH!
dialogue.line: The panel ripped free, plummeting towards Minjun. Time seemed to slow. I lunged forward, shoving him with all my might.

dialogue.speaker: Kang Minjun
dialogue.line: Oof! Jinwoo, what the hell?!

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: The panel smashed onto the floor where Minjun had been standing a split second before, scattering dust and debris. A collective gasp rippled through the hallway.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: Are you okay?

dialogue.speaker: Kang Minjun
dialogue.line: Yeah, I think so… What was that?

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: My hands were shaking. I'd saved him, just barely. But this wasn't how he died last time. This wasn't the truck. It was always something new, something unexpected. The killer wasn't a person, or an event. It was… fate itself.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: My gaze fell on a small, intricately carved wooden charm lying amidst the debris near the fallen panel. It wasn't there before. A chill ran down my spine.

dialogue.speaker: Lee Jinwoo
dialogue.line: No, I'm not giving up. I'll find out what's doing this. I have to.

summary: Lee Jinwoo wakes up from another time loop, haunted by his friend Minjun's latest death. He tries to subtly protect Minjun, only for a new, unexpected danger to emerge, barely averted, hinting at a deeper, more insidious threat.
